BofA, Goldman Sachs latest on Wall Street to lift S&P 500 index's annual target (Reuters) -BofA Global Research and Goldman Sachs on Tuesday became the latest Wall Street brokerages to raise their year-end targets for the S&P 500 index, broadly driven by reduced policy uncertainty, resilient corporate earnings and potential interest rate cuts.  BofA lifted the benchmark's index target to 6300 from 5600, while Goldman upped their target to 6600 from 6100, implying an upside of 1% and about 6% respectively to the last close of 6,229.28.
